title: calea ferruginea
intro: Calea ferruginea, also known as the Mexican Damiana or yerba de la pasion, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Calea ferruginea include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and enhance libido, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps. Calea ferruginea can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in some herbal remedies to support reproductive health and overall well-being.
